_note: TCE TriadDiscovery corpus encoding of the NS obstruction program (SPEC.md NS-001..040, re-encoded 2026-06-04 for the matured 30-entry ledger). Fed to the TCE engine via SpecBridge -> Discovery.Triadic.findTriadicCandidates (test-suite triadic-coordination-runonspec, SPEC_JSON env var).  FIELDS: name/kind/layer/logic/status/deps are read by the decoder; underscore-prefixed fields are human notes the decoder ignores.  DEPS are the genuine logical premises read off SPEC.md prose (e.g. NS-005 'critical norm = NS-002', NS-008 'energy(NS-003)+scaling(NS-002)', NS-010<->NS-004 BKM, NS-013<-NS-012+NS-010).  LAYER = depth in the program (problem=0 ... live frontier=5).  LOGIC TIER: classical = the PDE-analysis domain; other:closure = the closure/turbulence-model arc (a SEPARATE domain); bridge = NS-024/NS-030, the ONLY legal cross-tier links (faithful to their role as the witnessed/methodological connectors).  STATUS = 'proved_conditional' on EVERY entry is the engine's anchor-eligibility flag ONLY (the engine builds triads from isProved anchors); it reads 'established within its declared Scope/condition' and is WHOLLY DECOUPLED from the SPEC.md PDE-proof firewall, where :proved (unconditional, Scope:PDE) remains 0. No SPEC.md/registry status is altered by this file.
